metric_value: DB2, Oracle, Informix, Sybase, Microsoft SQL Server, IMS, VSAM
notes: DataJoiner supports distributed joins and queries across multiple database types from multiple suppliers

metric_value: Log-based capture to staging area then apply to destination; minimizes production database impact
notes: Log-based capture from DB2 and IMS; trigger-based from Oracle/Informix/Sybase; parallel replication to multiple targets

metric_value: DB2-to-Sybase, DB2-to-Oracle, Oracle-to-DB2, Oracle-to-Oracle confirmed customer deployments
notes: Customers have used DataPropagator and DataJoiner to replicate between these combinations

metric_value: Dynamic HTML/SQL macros for browser-to-backend-database interaction without bypassing web server
notes: Net.data supports native access to DB2, Oracle, Sybase; ODBC gateway; DB2 Extenders for multimedia; stored procedures

metric_value: State management via variable substitution for OLTP transactions across web connections
notes: Allows 'state' tracking for DB2 transactions between web connections — anticipating session/state management in web apps

metric_value: Full enterprise data integration is 'difficult if not impossible'; focused data marts achieve competitive advantage
notes: Key insight: 'paralysis by analysis' from attempting complete enterprise data dictionaries; smaller-scale data marts more practical

metric_value: Enterprises facing proliferating data archipelagoes requiring ever-faster merging of larger data streams
notes: Coined 'data archipelagoes' metaphor for fragmented distributed databases — prescient framing of modern data sprawl

metric_value: Key value: reuse information integration middleware across recurring projects vs. one-off builds
notes: 'Rather than creating or acquiring a new information-integration tool for each new project, IS can extend highly flexible tools'

metric_value: 1997 plans to add SQL-3 standard support and mimic advanced operations on DBMSs lacking them
notes: Planned DataJoiner support for SQL-3 features and simulation of advanced operations on non-supporting DBMSs

metric_value: Applications do not change when underlying architecture changes — open-server API features
notes: DataJoiner's open-server API provides location and network transparency

metric_value: Will enable 'write-once' apps accessing multiple legacy backend databases over Intranet
notes: Aberdeen predicts DataJoiner+Net.data will allow write-once applications across multiple legacy databases

metric_value: DataJoiner absorbed into IBM DB2 Information Integrator (~2002); later InfoSphere Federation Server
notes: Write-once multi-database access vision partially achieved; DataJoiner itself discontinued as standalone product. IBM InfoSphere carried the vision.

metric_value: Will add further support for traditional RDBMS scalability technologies such as cursors and governors
notes: Aberdeen anticipates further RDBMS scalability technology support in DataPropagator

metric_value: Absorbed into IBM DB2 Replication; log-based CDC pattern validated by entire industry
notes: DataPropagator integrated into DB2 replication. Log-based CDC became industry standard; Debezium, AWS DMS, Striim all use same pattern.

metric_value: Visual Programming Environment due next year (1998) for rapid development; further Java capabilities
notes: IBM plans a full-fledged Visual Programming Environment for Net.data circa 1998

metric_value: Net.data superseded by WebSphere Application Server and JSP/servlet model circa 2000-2002
notes: IBM WebSphere Application Server (1998+) replaced Net.data's niche. JSP/servlet became standard web-to-database pattern.

metric_value: DataPropagator's log-based architecture will deliver performance and isolation advantages in network computing
notes: Aberdeen highlights log-based capture as key differentiator: operates in 'virtual isolation'; minimizes performance overhead

metric_value: Log-based CDC became dominant pattern for enterprise data replication across all major cloud and on-premises tools
notes: Debezium, AWS Database Migration Service, Fivetran, Striim all use log-based CDC. DataPropagator's architecture was prescient.

metric_value: DB2 serves as hub for heterogeneous replication when combined with DataJoiner and DataPropagator
notes: Combined DataPropagator + DataJoiner + Net.data provides legacy leverage and write-once application capability

metric_value: Informix named as supported DataJoiner join target via open-server operations
notes: DataJoiner supports Informix joins; IBM acquired Informix in 2001

metric_value: Supports push from laptop or pull from central server for mobile/laptop replication
notes: For laptops in mobile networks, replication can be pushed from laptop or pulled from central server — anticipates mobile sync patterns

metric_value: Targets enterprises with multiple-supplier databases; positions reuse as competitive differentiator
notes: Products especially useful for enterprises with key databases from multiple suppliers, multiple types, or multiple platforms

metric_value: Outstanding example of IBM winning by the details; majority of enterprises should evaluate
notes: 'Outstanding example of IBM's ability to win by the details in an increasing strategic area of user needs'

metric_value: Sybase SQL Server supported as DataJoiner target; PowerBuilder can invoke DataJoiner
notes: DataJoiner supports Sybase; PowerBuilder can invoke DataJoiner from Sybase/Powersoft CADE

metric_value: Enables data movement and application location independence as part of information integration
notes: MQSeries provides store-and-forward messaging enabling data warehouse replication and distributed database architectures
